changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline
changeSet.comment: Local mirror of one Outline document per (workspace, connection, document_id).

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline
changeSet.comment: One row per Outline collection an admin chose to mirror (the Outline analog of slack_monitored_channel). Carries the human-facing catalog fields for the admin UI, the mirror lifecycle (ENABLED/PAUSED), and the per-collection sync bookkeeping: sync_status PENDING until a clean full pass completes, documents_synced_at for stalest-first ordering.
changeSet.sql: ALTER TABLE outline_collection ADD CONSTRAINT ck_outline_collection_state CHECK (state IN ('ENABLED', 'PAUSED')); ALTER TABLE outline_collection ADD CONSTRAINT ck_outline_collection_sync_status CHECK (sync_status IN ('PENDING', 'COMPLETE'));

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline
changeSet.comment: Document authorship substrate: the Outline user UUID + display name for creator and last editor. Subjects join identity_link lazily at projection time (no member FK — documents are mutable and re-synced, a stamped member id would go stale on late account links). No email column by design (email is never a lookup key).

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_constraint WHERE conname = 'ck_login_provider_type' AND pg_get_constraintdef(oid) LIKE '%OUTLINE%'

changeSet.comment: Allow Outline as a link-only OAuth login provider for account linking.

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_constraint WHERE conname = 'ck_connection_kind' AND pg_get_constraintdef(oid) LIKE '%OUTLINE%'

changeSet.comment: Allow OUTLINE Connection rows (the enum-backed kind column carries a CHECK the JPA tests never see).

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline
changeSet.comment: Research-fitness columns on the document mirror: outline_created_at (upstream createdAt — up-to-dateness signal), collaborator_subjects (JSONB array of Outline user UUIDs from Document.collaboratorIds — middle editors beyond creator/last-editor), body_evicted_at (stamped when the size cap evicts a body, cleared on re-export; the hash is KEPT so an evicted-but-unmodified document is never re-exported just to be evicted again).

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline
changeSet.comment: Per-collection coverage counters (RQ1): documents_upstream = |documents.list ∪ tree| seen at the last enumeration (clean or not), exports_skipped_for_budget = exports the last pass skipped when the shared budget ran out (0 on a clean pass). Mirrored-live stays derivable as a row count.

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline
changeSet.comment: Append-only per-document event log written from the webhook consumer (longitudinal editing-habit signal + middle editors). occurred_at is the envelope's createdAt (upstream clock), actor_subject the envelope's actorId. Webhook-forward only: no events.list backfill, no revisions.list bodies. Erased with the workspace/connection (actor subjects are personal data); document tombstones do NOT touch it — it is the audit trail of the mirror.

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline
changeSet.comment: Tenancy FKs for the Outline tables (sfk_* = scalar FK, per ADR 0017: DB-level constraint with no JPA association, excluded from the Hibernate drift diff). No DB cascade — the workspace purge contributor and the revoke erase paths remove rows explicitly; the FK guarantees no orphaned Outline content can outlive its workspace or connection.

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_constraint WHERE conname = 'ck_outline_document_tombstone'

changeSet.comment: Tombstone invariant: a document deleted upstream keeps only its structural marker — no body, no hash, no person-bearing subject may survive on a tombstoned row.
changeSet.rollback: ALTER TABLE outline_document DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS ck_outline_document_tombstone;

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline
changeSet.comment: Outline archive is soft/recoverable — distinct from deleted_at (hard tombstone, body wiped). archived_at marks a document archived in the wiki while KEEPING body/hash/authors, so a still-linked reference to an archived-but-recoverable document resolves to its real content, not an empty tombstone placeholder.

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline
changeSet.comment: Collection description as shown in Outline, captured server-side from collections.list (truncated to fit on write).

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline
changeSet.comment: Optimistic-lock guard: a webhook-triggered refreshDocument and a mid-flight full reconcile's per-document upsert both run in their own REQUIRES_NEW transaction with nothing serializing them per-workspace, so they can race the SAME row — without a version column a full-column save from the loser silently clobbers the winner's write. Defaults existing rows to 0 (a fresh row, not a conflict); OutlineDocumentSyncService retries a losing write once against the row's current version before leaving it for the next reconcile.

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline
changeSet.comment: Optimistic-lock guard: this row has two concurrent writers — the admin PATCH (state ENABLED/PAUSED) and the sync passes' bookkeeping (lastSyncError, watermarks, coverage counters) — and a registration or PAUSED→ENABLED resume kicks a sync off the request thread close enough in time to race the admin's own commit. Same defaulting rationale as outline_document.version.

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_indexes WHERE indexname = 'ix_outline_document_projection'

changeSet.comment: Hot-path indexes backing real finders that otherwise page-scan: findForProjection's ORDER BY deleted_at ASC NULLS FIRST, outline_updated_at DESC NULLS LAST, id ASC (the agent-facing document listing — the existing (workspace_id, outline_updated_at) index carries no deleted_at, so it cannot serve this compound sort; raw SQL is used because the exact NULLS placement matters and Liquibase's createIndex column tag cannot express it). This index's leading two columns (workspace_id, deleted_at) also exactly match deleteByWorkspaceIdAndDeletedAtBefore's WHERE clause, so no separate index is added for that finder. ix_outline_document_eviction is a partial index for findEvictionCandidates' ORDER BY last_materialized_at ASC NULLS FIRST over only rows that still hold a body — tombstoned/evicted rows the eviction walk never visits are excluded from the index entirely. ix_outline_document_slug backs findByWorkspaceIdAndReferenceIn's slug branch (the document_id branch is already covered by uk_outline_document).

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_indexes WHERE indexname = 'ix_outline_collection_pending_sync'

changeSet.comment: findDistinctWorkspaceIdsWithPendingSync is the catch-up tick's fan-out: an UNSCOPED, cross-tenant native query (state = 'ENABLED' AND sync_status = 'PENDING') that runs on every tick regardless of whether anything is actually pending, so without an index it full-scans outline_collection across every workspace on every run. A partial index on exactly that predicate keeps the common case (nothing pending) a near-empty-index probe instead of a growing full-table scan as the number of workspaces/collections grows.
changeSet.sql: CREATE INDEX ix_outline_collection_pending_sync ON outline_collection (workspace_id) WHERE state = 'ENABLED' AND sync_status = 'PENDING';

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_indexes WHERE indexname = 'ix_connection_outline_subscription'

changeSet.comment: Every Outline webhook delivery resolves its connection by the subscription id carried in the body, and that lookup happens BEFORE the HMAC is checked - an unauthenticated caller can reach it. Without an index the resolver walks every ACTIVE Outline connection, so the cost of a forged delivery grows with the number of connected workspaces. This makes the lookup a single indexed probe whose cost is independent of tenant count.

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_indexes WHERE indexname = 'ix_outline_document_fts'

changeSet.comment: Document retrieval ranks the mirror with to_tsvector/ts_rank. Unindexed, that rebuilds a tsvector for every live row on every mentor and review call. The expression matches the one in OutlineDocumentRepository#searchByRelevance exactly - including the left() bound, which is not an optimisation but a correctness guard: Postgres refuses a tsvector over 1MB, so a single large mirrored document would otherwise make the query throw for the whole workspace.

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline

preConditions.sqlCheck: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_indexes WHERE indexname = 'ix_outline_document_updated'

changeSet.comment: No query orders or filters on (workspace_id, outline_updated_at) - the projection read is served by ix_outline_document_projection and the search read by the GIN index above. The index only costs writes on the sync path.

rollback.sql: CREATE INDEX ix_outline_document_updated ON outline_document (workspace_id, outline_updated_at);

changeSet.author: hephaestus-outline
changeSet.comment: The provider table is identity_provider, not git_provider, and identity_link.provider_id was renamed with it. auth_event kept the old column name, leaving the schema half-renamed and the entity annotated against a name that no longer describes anything.
